ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly states 'Chi-Chi'. Art depicts a female-presenting fighter in blue and white martial arts uniform with dark hair pulled back — consistent with Chi-Chi's tournament-era appearance in Dragon Ball. Title 'Fateful Reunion' references her and Goku's reunion at the 23rd World Tournament.

trivia_bullets.text: Chi-Chi's canonical tournament debut is the 23rd World Martial Arts Tournament, where she competed under the alias 'Anonymous' (entry number 59 in the preliminaries). She and Goku faced each other in a match before Goku recognized her — this is the 'fateful reunion' the card title references. [Chi-Chi Dragon Ball Wiki, via WebSearch snippet]

Chi-Chi is the daughter of the Ox-King, wife of Goku, mother of Gohan and Goten, grandmother of Pan, and great-great-grandmother of Goku Jr. in the GT timeline — a 4-generation matrilineal lineage that anchors the Son family's earthly human side across the entire Dragon Ball franchise. [Wikipedia: Chi-Chi (Dragon Ball); CBR: Goku's Complete Family Tree]

Chi-Chi is a quarter-Saiyan great-grandmother of Pan: Goku (Saiyan) → Gohan (half-Saiyan) → Pan (quarter-Saiyan), with Chi-Chi as the human grounding force in each generational transition. Pan inherits the fighting spirit through both lines. [Wikipedia: Chi-Chi; Pan Dragon Ball Wiki, via WebSearch snippet]

This card is a Rare (TB2-037) in DBS-TB02, one rarity below the Super Rares like Jackie Chun (TB2-058) — reflecting Chi-Chi's status as a significant but non-tier-1 tournament fighter in the source material. [dbs-cardgame.com]

related_cards.name: Awakening Talent Pan
related_cards.note: Pan is Chi-Chi's granddaughter; 4-generation lineage node.

related_cards.name: Speed Attack Piccolo Jr.
related_cards.note: Piccolo mentored Gohan, Chi-Chi's son — the two primary adult influences on the next generation.
